text:
BMT OUTPATIENT CLINIC — ANNUAL FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Patient: Marcus T. Delgado
Attending: [Attending Hematologist/BMT Physician]
Visit Type: Routine post-transplant surveillance, 12-13 months post-allo-HSCT

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Mr. Delgado is a 49-year-old male with a history of de novo acute myeloid leukemia (AML, FAB M1, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H, ELN 2022 adverse risk) who presents today for his annual post-transplant surveillance visit, approximately 12-13 months following matched unrelated donor (MUD) allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplantation (allo-HSCT). He is doing well overall and has no new complaints.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Mr. Delgado was diagnosed with de novo AML approximately 14 months ago after presenting with a 3-week history of progressive fatigue, pallor, gingival hypertrophy with spontaneous bleeding, and epistaxis. At diagnosis, his WBC was markedly elevated at 84,000/uL with 62% circulating blasts. Bone marrow biopsy confirmed AML with 78% blasts, normal diploid karyotype (46,XY), and NGS identified a FLT3-ITD with a high allelic ratio of 0.87, co-occurring with a DNMT3A R882H mutation — conferring ELN 2022 adverse-risk classification.

He received standard 7+3 induction (cytarabine 100 mg/m2/day continuous infusion days 1-7 and daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 IV days 1-3) with midostaurin 50 mg PO BID days 8-21 per the RATIFY protocol. His course was complicated by febrile neutropenia (cultures negative), grade 2 oral mucositis, and grade 2 nausea requiring temporary midostaurin hold. He achieved complete morphologic remission after a single cycle of induction, with MRD negativity by 10-color multiparameter flow cytometry (sensitivity 10^-4) and FLT3-ITD PCR negativity confirmed on the day +28-30 bone marrow assessment.

Given the adverse-risk biology, he proceeded directly to myeloablative allogeneic transplant in CR1 without intervening consolidation, approximately 9 weeks from diagnosis. A 10/10 HLA-matched unrelated donor was identified through the NMDP (male, CMV seropositive). Conditioning was FluBu4 (fludarabine 30 mg/m2/day x5 days, busulfan 3.2 mg/kg/day x4 days IV with pharmacokinetic targeting). GVHD prophylaxis was tacrolimus plus short-course MTX. Peripheral blood stem cells were infused on day 0 (CD34+ dose 5.8 x 10^6/kg). Engraftment was achieved (neutrophil day +14, platelet day +19), complicated by grade 2 mucositis requiring temporary PCA morphine and supplemental tube feeds, febrile neutropenia (blood cultures negative), and mild tacrolimus-associated nephrotoxicity (grade 1, creatinine peak 1.4 mg/dL, responsive to dose reduction).

Post-transplant course has been notable for mild chronic GVHD involving skin (bilateral palms, erythema and scaling, NIH score 1) and oral mucosa (lichenoid changes of bilateral buccal mucosa, NIH score 1, no symptoms interfering with eating), which was managed with topical triamcinolone and budesonide oral rinse respectively. No systemic corticosteroids were ever required. Tacrolimus was successfully tapered and discontinued at approximately 8 months post-transplant without GVHD flare. Follow-up bone marrow biopsy at day +180 confirmed ongoing complete morphologic remission, MRD negativity by flow and digital droplet PCR for DNMT3A R882H, FLT3-ITD PCR negativity, and full donor chimerism (whole blood 99% donor, myeloid 100% donor, T-cell 96% donor). Surveillance CT imaging at the 12-month mark showed no evidence of extramedullary disease or AML recurrence.

At today's visit, the patient reports he has returned to full-time work as a high school science teacher and has fully resumed recreational activities including recreational basketball. He has no new constitutional symptoms, no fever, no night sweats, no unexplained weight loss, no lymphadenopathy, no bone pain, and no new skin or mucosal complaints. Chronic GVHD of skin and oral mucosa has fully resolved clinically. He is not currently on any immunosuppression.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: No fever, chills, or drenching night sweats. No unintentional weight loss. Energy levels reported as "back to baseline." No significant fatigue.
HEENT: No oral soreness, no difficulty chewing or swallowing. No dry or gritty eyes. No photophobia. No nasal congestion or epistaxis.
Pulmonary: No cough, no dyspnea on exertion, no wheezing.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, no palpitations, no lower extremity edema.
Gastrointestinal: No nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, or abdominal pain. No hematochezia or melena. Regular bowel habits.
Genitourinary: No hematuria, no dysuria, no nocturia beyond baseline.
Musculoskeletal: No joint pain, no new arthralgias, no muscle cramps.
Skin: Patient notes bilateral palms are at baseline without erythema or scaling. No new rashes.
Neurologic: No new cognitive symptoms, no peripheral neuropathy, no headache.
Endocrine: Patient notes mild heat intolerance — has not seen endocrinology yet, appointment pending. No symptoms to suggest frank hypothyroidism or hypogonadism beyond this.
Hematologic/lymphatic: No easy bruising, no petechiae, no spontaneous bleeding.
Psychiatric: Reports good mood, good adjustment to survivorship. Denies depression or anxiety. Declined formal psychosocial referral at prior visit.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

Vital signs: Temperature 36.8 C, BP 126/78 mmHg, HR 68 bpm, RR 14, O2 saturation 99% on room air. Weight 83.5 kg (BMI 24.1, stable from prior visit, no significant weight change).

General: Well-appearing, well-nourished male in no acute distress. Alert and oriented. Conversational, relaxed, pleasant affect.

Head/Eyes/Ears/Nose/Throat: Pupils equal and reactive to light. Sclera anicteric. No conjunctival pallor. Oropharynx: oral mucosa moist and intact. The previously noted lichenoid changes of bilateral buccal mucosa are no longer visible. No erythema, no pseudomembrane, no ulceration. Dentition intact.

Neck: No cervical, submandibular, or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy. Thyroid: symmetric, no palpable nodule, not enlarged.

Chest/Pulmonary: Symmetric expansion. Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, rhonchi, or rales.

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. Bowel sounds present and normoactive. Liver edge not palpable. Spleen not palpable. No palpable abdominal masses. No inguinal lymphadenopathy.

Skin: Bilateral palms and dorsal hands: no erythema, no scaling, no sclerodermatous changes. No rash on trunk or extremities. No petechiae or purpura. No jaundice. No hyperpigmentation consistent with cGVHD.

Extremities: No clubbing, cyanosis, or edema. Full range of motion of upper and lower extremities. No joint swelling.

Neurological: Alert and fully oriented. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. No focal motor or sensory deficits. Gait normal. No fine tremor (tacrolimus discontinued month 8).

Lymph nodes: No palpable lymphadenopathy in cervical, axillary, inguinal, or femoral chains.

Performance status: ECOG 0.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS

Complete blood count:
- WBC: 7.4 k/uL (normal)
- ANC: 5,100/uL (normal)
- Hemoglobin: 14.0 g/dL (normal)
- MCV: 91 fL
- Platelets: 231,000/uL (normal)
- Differential: neutrophils 69%, lymphocytes 22%, monocytes 7%, eosinophils 1.5%, basophils 0.5%; no circulating blasts identified

Comprehensive metabolic panel:
- Sodium 139 mEq/L, Potassium 4.1 mEq/L, Chloride 102 mEq/L, Bicarbonate 26 mEq/L
- BUN 14 mg/dL, Creatinine 0.88 mg/dL (GFR >80, improved from prior tacrolimus-related elevation)
- Glucose 94 mg/dL
- Total bilirubin 0.6 mg/dL (normal)
- AST 28 U/L (normal), ALT 32 U/L (normal), Alkaline phosphatase 74 U/L (normal)
- Total protein 7.2 g/dL, Albumin 4.3 g/dL

LFTs normalized completely — the mild grade 1 transaminase elevations seen in the context of possible early hepatic cGVHD (AST 42, ALT 48 at day +60 to +90) have since resolved.

Additional labs sent today:
- Peripheral blood FLT3-ITD PCR: NEGATIVE (result confirmed at today's visit, consistent with prior serial testing)
- CMV PCR peripheral blood: undetectable
- EBV PCR peripheral blood: undetectable
- Tacrolimus level: not applicable (off drug)
- Thyroid-stimulating hormone (TSH): pending (ordered today given reported mild heat intolerance)
- Testosterone (total), FSH, LH: pending (ordered today, baseline gonadal assessment as part of late effects screening)
- Lipid panel: ordered today (late transplant metabolic screening)
- Fasting glucose and hemoglobin A1c: ordered today

---

IMAGING RESULTS

CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with IV contrast (12-month post-transplant surveillance scan, reviewed at today's visit): No evidence of lymphadenopathy in the chest, abdomen, or pelvis. Lungs clear without pulmonary nodules, consolidation, or effusion. Liver and spleen both normal in size. No focal hepatic lesions. Osseous structures show homogeneous marrow signal with no focal lytic or blastic lesions and no evidence of marrow replacement. Overall: entirely unremarkable post-transplant imaging, no evidence of recurrent or extramedullary AML.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. AML (FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H, ELN adverse risk) — SUSTAINED COMPLETE REMISSION, 12-13 months post-MUD allo-HSCT.

Mr. Delgado continues to do extremely well. He is 12-13 months out from a 10/10 MUD allo-HSCT in CR1, with serially negative MRD by flow cytometry and FLT3-ITD PCR, full donor chimerism confirmed at day +180, and normal CBC and metabolic labs today. The 12-month surveillance CT scan showed no evidence of extramedullary disease or recurrence. The combination of MRD-negative CR1 at transplant, full engraftment and full donor chimerism, and absence of relapse at the 12-month mark is reassuring. For ELN adverse-risk AML transplanted in MRD-negative CR1, the estimated 2-year DFS in published series is approximately 50-60%, and this patient's course to date has been favorable.

We reviewed today's FLT3-ITD PCR result — NEGATIVE — which is highly reassuring and consistent with his ongoing deep molecular remission.

Plan:
- Continue quarterly outpatient surveillance visits with CBC and metabolic panel
- Next formal bone marrow biopsy with chimerism and MRD assessment planned at 24 months post-transplant (approximately 11 months from today), per protocol
- Peripheral blood FLT3-ITD PCR to be repeated at each quarterly visit
- Continue surveillance CT imaging annually; next scan at approximately 24 months post-transplant
- No antileukemic treatment indicated at this time

2. Chronic GVHD — FULLY RESOLVED.

The mild chronic GVHD affecting skin (bilateral palms, NIH grade 1) and oral mucosa (buccal lichenoid changes, NIH grade 1) that was present from day +45 through approximately month 6-8 has completely resolved clinically. Tacrolimus was discontinued at month 8 without flare. LFTs have normalized. Physical exam today shows no residual skin or mucosal GVHD manifestations. No ocular, pulmonary, gastrointestinal, or sclerodermatous GVHD was ever identified.

Plan:
- No current GVHD-directed therapy
- Patient counseled to report any new skin, oral, ocular, pulmonary, or GI symptoms promptly
- Annual pulmonary function testing (PFTs) recommended given transplant history; will order for next visit

3. Late transplant effects — ONGOING SCREENING.

Given the patient's age (49 years), prior myeloablative conditioning (FluBu4, gonadotoxic and potentially thyrotoxic), and use of tacrolimus, the following late-effects screening is in progress:

- Bone density: DEXA scan ordered at last visit; results pending, to be reviewed at next visit. Will refer to endocrinology if evidence of osteopenia/osteoporosis; counsel on calcium and vitamin D supplementation in the interim.
- Thyroid: TSH ordered today given reported mild heat intolerance. Will evaluate results and refer to endocrinology if abnormal. Busulfan-based conditioning carries a risk of thyroid dysfunction.
- Gonadal function: Testosterone, FSH, LH ordered today. Patient is aware that myeloablative conditioning may have affected gonadal function. Fertility preservation was not undertaken prior to treatment (patient was already father to two children and did not pursue banking). Will refer to endocrinology for gonadal axis management if hypogonadism is confirmed.
- Cataract screening: Ophthalmology referral placed today. Busulfan-based conditioning and prior corticosteroid exposure (dexamethasone during induction) associated with posterior subcapsular cataract risk.
- Metabolic/cardiovascular: Lipid panel, fasting glucose, and HbA1c ordered today. Tacrolimus-related metabolic effects (dyslipidemia, glucose intolerance) can persist. Will manage any abnormalities in conjunction with primary care.
- Secondary malignancy counseling: Patient counseled that transplant recipients carry a modestly elevated lifetime risk for secondary solid tumors and post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD). Importance of annual skin examination, colorectal cancer screening (family history of colon cancer, paternal [maternal] uncle age 62), and avoidance of HPV-related exposures discussed. Screening colonoscopy discussed (per family history, consider earlier initiation than standard guidelines); referral to gastroenterology placed.
- Sun protection counseling reinforced: chronic UV exposure risk after conditioning; broad-spectrum SPF 50 recommended.

4. Infection prophylaxis — CONTINUING THROUGH MONTH 12.

Patient is currently on:
- Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (PCP prophylaxis): continue through at least 12 months post-transplant and until immunosuppression fully cleared (already off tacrolimus; TMP-SMX will be continued for a defined period per institutional protocol)
- Acyclovir (HSV/VZV prophylaxis): continue through 12 months post-transplant, then transition to PRN or discontinue per protocol
- Fluconazole: discussed discontinuation at this visit given stable engraftment and no current immunosuppression; will taper off per institutional guidelines

Plan: Discuss with pharmacist and confirm timing for anti-infective discontinuation at next visit.

5. Vaccinations — IN PROGRESS.

Patient received inactivated influenza, pneumococcal polysaccharide (PPSV23), and Tdap at approximately 6 months post-transplant. Reviewed today and upcoming vaccination schedule:
- PCV13 (pneumococcal conjugate) due at approximately 12 months: ordered today
- Inactivated influenza: annual, due at next fall season
- Hepatitis B series: to be completed; verify titers
- Live vaccines (MMR, varicella): deferred until minimum 24 months post-transplant and off immunosuppression for >12 months

6. Hypertension — STABLE.

Patient continues on lisinopril for well-controlled hypertension (BP 126/78 today). No changes to antihypertensive regimen. Monitoring adequate through primary care.

7. Psychosocial / survivorship.

Patient has adjusted well. He has returned to full-time teaching and his recreational activities. He declined formal psychosocial referral at last visit and again today, stating he feels well and has support from family. He was encouraged to utilize the survivorship program and resources as needed. A formal survivorship care plan document will be prepared and provided at next visit, summarizing treatment history, late effects, and recommended ongoing surveillance for coordination with his primary care physician.

Return to clinic: Approximately 3 months for routine follow-up, CBC, metabolic panel, and peripheral blood FLT3-ITD PCR. Review pending labs (TSH, testosterone, LH/FSH, lipid panel, HbA1c, DEXA results) at next visit.

All questions addressed. Patient verbalized understanding and agreement with plan.

— [Attending Hematologist/BMT Physician, Hematology/Oncology]
[Fellow/PA co-signature as applicable]
